documentation.description: The simple Dec PDP-11 simulator inheriting from Sim.simulator.

simple_types.code:
type reg_id is (reg_r0,
                reg_r1,
                reg_r2,
                reg_r3,

                reg_usp,
                reg_ksp,
                …,

documentation.description: Record for exception information

simple_types.name: variants_pdp11
simple_types.code:
type variants_pdp11 is (var_1110,
                        var_1120,
                        var_1104,
                        var_1134,
                        var_1140);

documentation.description:
Features for each processor.  Eventually, an array of these may be created
with entries for each supported processor.  This may also be extended to
include information for other differences between various models.

Extra instructions (not EIS) are included on all but the earliest models
(probably not available on PDP-11/04, /05, /10, /15, and /20).  Instructions
in this set are:
SXT, XOR, MARK, SOB, and RTT (PDP-11/04 has RTT, but not others).

Having a MMU adds the following for a basic MMU.
MFPI, MTPI, SPL (maybe specific to PDP-11/45)

Extended instruction set is an option for PDP-11/35, /40, and /03 CPUs and
standard on later models, except PDP-11/04 (probably not available on PDP-11/05,
/10, /15, and /20).  Instuctions in this set are:
MUL, DIV, ASH, and ASHC.

FIS is available only on PDP-11/34 and /40.  Instructions in this set are:
FADD, FSUB, FMUL, and FDIV

FPP is available on PDP-11/45, /70 and probably at least an option on all
later models.  Instructions in this set are:
CFCC, SETF, SETI, SETDF, SETL, LDFPS, STFPS, STST, CLR[FD], TST[FD],
ABS[FD], NEG[FD], MUL[FD], MOD[FD], ADD[FD], LD[FD], SUB[FD], CMP[FD],
ST[FD], DIV[FD], STEXP, STC[FD][IL], STCFD, STCDF, LDEXP, LDC[IL][FD],
LDCDF, and LDCFD

CIS is available on PDP-11/23, /24, /44, and /74?.

MTPS and MFPS may only be available on the PDP-11/34.

record_types.name: ex_info

documentation.description:
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Simulator control

Called first to initialize the simulator

fields.name: timeout
fields.description: Number of instructions to execute before interrupt can be recognized.

record_types.name: features

fields.name: has_extra
fields.description: Has extra instructions (not EIS - STX, MARK, SOB, XOR)

fields.name: has_RTT
fields.description: Has the RTT instruction (11/04 has this, but not the rest of extras)

fields.name: has_EIS
fields.description: Has extended instruction set (EIS - MUL, DIV, ASH, ASHC)

fields.name: has_ID
fields.description: Has separate I and D addressing

fields.name: set_PSW_T
fields.description: T bit in PSW can be set by directly writing to it

fields.name: reg_value
fields.description: In OP Rx,-(Rx)+ type instructions, use original value of Rx

fields.name: reg_bus
fields.description: Registers can be read at certain unibus addresses

fields.name: stack_limit
fields.description: Cause a trap when SP is below this value

tagged_types.name: pdp11
tagged_types.code: type pdp11 is new simulator with private;

documentation.description:
The trace level is interpreted as follows for this simulator:
Bit  Use
 0   List instructions being traced
 1   List I/O operations
 2   List data operations
 3   List bus specific items.
 4   Unused
 5   Unused
 6   Unused
 7   Unused

Variants of processor
Note that some model numbers are for OEM versions of the processor
and are identical to another model number.  The OEM versions are not
considered to be separate variants.  It is likely that only some models
will be included in the simulation.

  DEC         OEM      Processor
PDP-11/20 = PDP-11/15  KA11/KC11
PDP-11/10 = PDP-11/05  KD11B
PDP-11/40 = PDP-11/35  KD11A

In addition, some CPUs are given different model number depending on whether
they have a Unibus or QBus.

Unibus      QBus
PDP-11/24 = PDP-11/23

access_types.name: pdp11_access
access_types.code: type pdp11_access is access all pdp11'Class;

constants.name: PDP_1104_feature
constants.code:
PDP_1104_feature : constant features := (has_extra => False, has_RTT => True, has_EIS => False,
                                         has_FIS => False, has_FPP => False, has_CIS => False,
                                         has_user => False, has_super => False, has_ID => False,
                                         has_MMU18 => False, has_MMU22 => False, has_MTFPS => False,
                                         SWAB_V => True, set_PSW_T => False, reg_value => True, reg_bus => True,
                                         stack_limit => 8#400#);
